title: Trace mark-source fallback frequency and guard-message payload origin
one_sentence: Determine exactly where mark_source is set, what condition triggers rest_fallback, how often fallback is occurring, and where the guard/warning text originates in the payload chain before it reaches the dashboard UI.
prior_task: DASHBOARD_MARK_SOURCE_AND_GUARD_MESSAGE_AUDIT_001 (PARTIAL PASS) — dashboard layer cleared; upstream origin not traced
allowed_files: bot_core.py (Step 5 only, guard payload write path only — justify if used)
forbidden_files: dashboard.html (already audited — do not re-read unless a specific new question requires it)
key_questions: Q1: What exact function/condition in dashboard_server.py sets mark_source per position?
Q2: What staleness threshold or stream-health check triggers rest_fallback?
Q3: Current fallback severity — rarely/intermittent/too often/main source? (a/b/c/d/e)
Q4: What writes last_guard_result / guard_reasons to the payload — bot_core or dashboard_server?
Q5: Is any 'max down' wording present in state.json, /api payload, or logs right now?
Q6: Root-cause classification for both symptoms
acceptance_criteria: Exact function + condition in dashboard_server.py that sets mark_source
Explicit staleness/stream-health trigger condition for rest_fallback
Severity label (a/b/c/d/e) for current fallback frequency with evidence
Guard/warning payload write chain traced to source
Explicit answer on whether 'max down' text is present anywhere in current runtime state
